interpretation: The European Union sacred political order demonstrates high corrigibility through its constitutive pluralist architecture: the TEU's 'ever closer union' frames integration as an open-ended process rather than a sacred destination, and its grounding in liberty, democracy, and rule of law provides normative self-correction mechanisms. Unlike the high-pathology cases, the EU's sacred political order cannot generate a sacred enemy from within its own structure — its legitimacy depends on multi-state consensus and legal adjudication. Democratic-backsliding episodes (Hungary, Poland) and Brexit test this corrigibility but also activate the order's correction mechanisms (Article 7, infringement procedures), supporting rather than undermining the corrigibility score.

confidence.label: moderate
confidence.rationale: Chain thin. Founding document passages establish the design intent; operational corrigibility requires expansion. Democratic-backsliding episodes introduce uncertainty.

mechanismSummary: The EU's multi-state, open-ended integration architecture structurally opposes sacred-enemy escalation and sacrificial demands, producing high corrigibility through consensus-building and legal adjudication rather than symbolic unification around a common threat.
